4, Power Armor: PDQ-R&D-34-98 (designer tunknown: salvaged from abandoned Old Reckoning armed forces base): This is abs Solutely effective; the only problem is that it ‘weighs one ton and occasionally falls through the flooring of the complex. It is also credibly slow and poorly maneuverable. Iti ait balky in first gear, a8 PCs will note in test runs; in the field, twill sometimes refuse to move forward uniess coaxed, (Roll 1-8 to statin fist gear, Other problems: the armor can slow only by one gear per turn; itis tunstable in third gear.) Performan Ist gear Stroll (oll 1-8 to start) 2nd gear Walk 3rd gear Run (1-10 for steering ron) The armor provides ALL protection. The power armor has a builtin blaster that fires along arm; the blaster fring button is con- Cealed inside a finger of one glove. As you might well expect, the firing button gets stuck in the firing position the third time its used.
